burial.plot_notes: Saegertown Cemetery, Saegertown Crawford County PA.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Ruth Lybarger (I00678) — wife of 59 years per obit. Full maiden name undocumented. Daughter Cynthia Lybarger predeceased Robert.

education.notes: Class of 1949 — same school sister Ruth attended (Class of 1943).

career.notes: 35 years of service at Saegertown Manufacturing, retiring 1996 as Plant Manager.

military_service.notes: U.S. Navy: entered Jan 5, 1951; honorable discharge Oct 25, 1954. Korean War-era service.

narrative_blocks.body: Robert entered the U.S. Navy on January 5, 1951 and served until his honorable discharge on October 25, 1954. He then settled in Saegertown Pennsylvania and spent thirty-five years at Saegertown Manufacturing, retiring in 1996 as Plant Manager. He was a member of Christ Evangelical Free Church Saegertown for the second half of his life.

narrative_blocks.header: Fifty-nine year marriage, daughter predeceased him
narrative_blocks.body: Robert married Ruth in 1953 — they were married for fifty-nine years until his death in 2012. Their daughter Cynthia Lybarger predeceased him at some point. He was also predeceased by his older brother Leroy 'Lee' Lybarger and his older sister Ruby Sheldon, leaving him survived only by oldest sister Ruth Watson and brother James in Texas.

burial.plot_notes: Saegertown Cemetery, Saegertown Crawford County PA.

He was also predeceased by his older brother Leroy 'Lee' Lybarger and his older sister Ruby Sheldon, leaving him survived only by oldest sister Ruth Watson and brother James in Texas.

burial.plot_notes: Roselawn Memorial Gardens, Meadville Crawford County PA.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Minerva Irene McKinnon Lybarger (I00676). Per James Donald's 2015 obituary: 'born to Freeman Wilbur Lybarger and Minerva Irene McKinnon.' At least 5 documented children: Ruth, Ruby, James Donald, Robert A, Leroy 'Lee' — to be built Stage 6Y-94.

military_service.notes: World War II veteran per FA 'Veteran' designation. Specific unit, rank, branch and theater of service undocumented in available sources.

naming_inspiration.description: First name Freeman honors his father Freeman Delbert Lybarger, who died at thirty-nine when this Freeman was just seven years old.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost father at seven, carried his name forward
narrative_blocks.body: Freeman Wilbur Lybarger was seven years old when his father Freeman Delbert died at thirty-nine in 1913. As the elder son he carried his father's first name forward into the next generation and into his own World War II service. His mother Bertha would remarry Silas Young twelve years later in 1925, giving him Young half-siblings when he was already nineteen.

narrative_blocks.header: Five children scattered across America in turn
narrative_blocks.body: Freeman Wilbur and Minerva McKinnon had at least five known children born 1925 to 1933: daughters Ruth and Ruby, sons James Donald (Korean War Navy veteran), Robert (Navy veteran), and Leroy 'Lee.' James eventually moved to Texas where he died in Killeen in 2015. The other siblings remained in Crawford County Pennsylvania.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA — with father-in-law Harmon Unger's Greendale-buried Ellis-Unger family.

narrative_blocks.header: Minnesota-born like his wife's late father Harmon
narrative_blocks.body: Freeman Delbert Lybarger was born April 14, 1874 in Fairfield Swift County Minnesota — by uncanny coincidence the same state where his wife Bertha's father Harmon Demmington Unger had been born seventeen years earlier. Both Minnesota-born men migrated east to Crawford County Pennsylvania, married into the Ellis-Unger family, and died young in Pennsylvania. Freeman died at thirty-nine in Warren PA on September 20, 1913.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA — body returned from Florida.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Freeman Delbert Lybarger (I00671, 1874 MN - 1913 PA). Two confirmed sons: Freeman Wilbur Lybarger (1906-1988, WWII vet), Irvin Clarence Lybarger (1908-1973). Per Gertrude Winger's 2014 obit there may have been additional siblings: 'Chester, Irvin, and Wilbur Lybarger' — Chester (and possibly others) to be researched. Children built later stages.

Silas Howard Young (I00672, 1873-1953). Multiple children — confirmed Gertrude Virginia Young Winger (1926-2014); per Gertrude's obit also Joseph, Noble, Leah, Austin, Reno, David Young, Lois Stanley, Paul Young, Dortha Free. Children to be built later stages.

narrative_blocks.header: Twice widowed, ended life far from home
narrative_blocks.body: Bertha Mae Unger was widowed twice — first when Freeman Delbert Lybarger died in 1913 at thirty-nine, then when second husband Silas Howard Young died in 1953 after twenty-eight years of marriage. She moved south to Florida in her later years and died in Hollywood Broward County in 1959 at seventy-four. Her body was returned to Meadville and buried at Greendale Cemetery.

narrative_blocks.header: Two husbands, many children across both marriages
narrative_blocks.body: Bertha's first marriage to Freeman Lybarger produced at least two confirmed sons before his 1913 death — Freeman Wilbur (1906) and Irvin Clarence (1908). Her second marriage to Silas Young in 1925 produced multiple Young children including Gertrude Virginia born 1926. Per a later family obituary at least ten siblings spanned the two marriages — many remain unbuilt pending research.

burialCemetery.name: Saegertown Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Saegertown Cemetery, Saegertown Crawford County PA. Burial site of Robert A. Lybarger (HD4286), Navy veteran and 35-year Plant Manager at Saegertown Manufacturing.
